text: Talk 1 lists five of six formats in parenthetical (missing Instruction-Matched English)
'Every multi-agent framework routes tasks through English or JSON' is an unsupported universal claim
'Natural language and JSON break as agent protocols' overstates findings — data shows tradeoffs not blanket failure
p < 0.001 cited without noting Holm-corrected pairwise is only marginal (p=0.063) — selective reporting risk
'115+ critique points' is unanchored — not demonstrated in experimental results, only asserted in debate context
'Neither model found through self-review alone' is asserted not demonstrated — no computed self-review catch rate
Both talks rooted in one project — double-dipping perception risk with CFP committee
'Matched English on compactness' needs caveat — free English significantly better on log(tok/unit) in mixed model
32% vs ~42% figures can confuse reviewers unless metric (tok/unit vs raw tokens) is labeled
11% failure rate needs denominator and context (9/81, one model) or it looks overstated
12 bugs weakly informative without severity classes
'Confirmed across three independent statistical approaches' is a new overclaim — Holm-corrected pairwise is marginal
Removing stats without acknowledging hybrid scoring and pending cross-validation leaves credibility gap
